content.text: CO‑OWNERSHIP AGREEMENT
Agreement ID: {agreementId}
Date: {agreementDate}
This Co‑Ownership Agreement ("Agreement") is made on this {agreementDate} between the parties listed below (each a "Co‑owner" and together the "Co‑owners").
PARTIES
Number of Co‑owners: {numberOfInvestors}. The Co‑owners are identified as follows (summary): {investorsShort}.
Full particulars of Co‑owners:
{investorsBlock}
RECITALS
A. The Co‑owners have agreed to purchase the immovable property located at {propertyLocationText} (the "Property") for a total consideration of INR {propertyValue} (the "Purchase Price").
B. The Co‑owners wish to record their respective contributions, equity interests, rights, duties and remedies in respect of the Property on the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement.
NOW IT IS AGREED as follows:
1. DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION

1.1 Definitions. In this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires: (a) "Applicable Law" means all laws, rules, regulations, orders, notifications and statutory requirements in force in India from time to time; (b) "Equity Percentage" means the percentage interest of a Co‑owner in the Property as set opposite their name in Clause 2.1; (c) "Net Rent" means gross rent received from the Property less only actual and reasonable out‑of‑pocket expenses necessarily incurred in relation to the generation of such rent and excluding taxes and statutory dues borne by the registered owner except as expressly agreed; and (d) "Transfer" includes sale, assignment, gift, pledge, mortgage, charge or other disposition.
1.2 Interpretation. Headings are for convenience only. References to clauses and schedules are to those in this Agreement.
2. CAPITAL CONTRIBUTIONS; TITLE; OWNERSHIP PERCENTAGES
2.1 Contributions and Equity. The Co‑owners’ initial contributions and respective Equity Percentages are as follows: {investorsBlock}.

2.2 Title. The legal title to the Property shall be registered in the name(s) of the person(s) recorded on title. The Parties agree that beneficial ownership shall be in proportion to the Equity Percentages, subject to the terms of this Agreement.

2.3 Changes to Equity. Any change to the Equity Percentage of any Co‑owner shall require a written deed, signed and (if required by law) registered, and shall be effective only when such deed is executed and statutory requirements (including stamp duty and registration) are complied with.
3. PURCHASE, STAMP DUTY, REGISTRATION AND COSTS

3.1 Purchase Payment. The Parties confirm that the Purchase Price has been/will be paid in accordance with the contributions set out in Clause 2.1. Any shortfall in payment required for completion shall be met as mutually agreed (for example, pro rata call on Co‑owners or loan).

3.2 Stamp Duty and Registration. Initial stamp duty/registration for the sale/transfer of the Property shall be borne by the purchaser/homebuyer unless otherwise agreed. Stamp duty/registration for any Transfer under Clause 7 shall be borne by the transferring Co‑owner unless otherwise agreed in writing.

3.3 Ongoing Costs. All ongoing property related costs including municipal taxes, utilities, insurance premiums, and other statutory levies and upkeep costs shall be allocated and paid in accordance with Clause 4.
4. MANAGEMENT, MAINTENANCE AND EXPENSES

4.1 Managing Owner. The Co‑owners hereby nominate/appoint {managingOwnerName} as the Managing Owner (the "Managing Owner") to manage day‑to‑day upkeep, tenancy arrangements, contracts for repairs and collection of rent. The Managing Owner’s powers include negotiation and execution of tenancy agreements (within mutually agreed duration), supervision of maintenance and arranging insurance.

4.2 Management Costs. All reasonable costs incurred in connection with the management and maintenance of the Property shall be paid from the Property’s funds where available; otherwise, such costs shall be borne by the Co‑owners in proportion to their Equity Percentages.

4.3 Monthly Accounts & Records. The Managing Owner shall maintain proper books and deliver a monthly statement within fourteen (14) days after the end of each month showing gross rent received, permitted deductions, Net Rent distribution and expenses.

4.4 Repair and Capital Expenditure. Ordinary repairs and maintenance shall be funded from Property receipts or contributions pro rata. Capital improvements above a reasonable threshold shall require prior unanimous consent of Co‑owners, save for emergencies threatening safety, in which case prompt notice shall be provided.

4.5 Insurance. The Managing Owner shall maintain adequate insurance (including fire and allied perils and third‑party liability) and provide copies of policies on request. Premiums shall be paid from Property funds or by Co‑owners pro rata.
5. RENT, PROFITS AND LOSSES
5.1 Rent Distribution. Net Rent shall be distributed among the Co‑owners monthly in proportion to their Equity Percentages, within seven (7) business days of receipt.

5.2 Reserves. The Managing Owner may retain a reasonable reserve fund from rents for anticipated repairs, tax payments or rent defaults; any retained reserves shall be reflected in monthly statements and accounted for in subsequent distribution.

5.3 Losses. Any loss attributable to the Property (including uninsured loss or diminution of value) shall be borne by Co‑owners in proportion to Equity Percentages, unless caused by the fraud, wilful default or gross negligence of a Co‑owner, in which case the defaulting Co‑owner shall be solely responsible.

5.4 Taxation. Each Co‑owner shall be individually responsible for declaring and paying tax on his/her share of rental income in accordance with Applicable Law. The Managing Owner shall provide necessary details and statements to enable correct tax filings. If tax is required to be deducted at source, the payer shall deduct and furnish the TDS certificate.
6. BANK ACCOUNTS AND PAYMENTS

6.1 Joint Account. The Co‑owners shall maintain a bank account in the name of the Property (the "Property Account") into which all rents, deposits and receipts shall be paid. Withdrawals shall follow agreed signing rules. All distributions to Co‑owners shall be made by bank transfer to the bank account details provided by each Co‑owner.
6.2 Reconciliation. The Managing Owner shall reconcile the Property Account monthly and provide statements to all Co‑owners.
7. EXIT, TRANSFER, PRE‑EMPTION AND BUY‑OUT
7.1 Voluntary Transfer. A Co‑owner desiring to Transfer his/her interest shall notify the other Co‑owners in writing specifying particulars and the intended price and terms.

7.2 Right of First Refusal/Pre‑emption. The remaining Co‑owners shall have a right of first refusal to acquire the Transferring Co‑owner’s interest on the same terms; if multiple elect to purchase, purchase shall be in proportion to Equity Percentages unless otherwise agreed; failing exercise within thirty (30) days, sale to a bona fide third party may proceed on no more favourable terms within a limited period.

7.3 Valuation for Transfer/Buy‑Out. Failing agreement, fair market value shall be determined by independent registered valuer(s) through a mutually agreed or three‑valuer mechanism; the resulting valuation shall be binding.

7.4 Buy‑Out. If a pre‑agreed buy‑out formula exists, it shall apply; otherwise, the Buy‑Out Price shall be the Equity Percentage of the Fair Market Value less any outstanding liabilities attributable to the Transferring Co‑owner.

7.5 Sale Mechanics. On payment of the agreed Buy‑Out Price and completion of necessary documentation, the Transferring Co‑owner shall transfer and assign his/her rights and interest to the purchasing Co‑owner(s) or third party and deliver possession, documents and indemnities as customary.

7.6 Forced Sale. If the Co‑owners unanimously agree, the Property may be sold and net proceeds distributed in accordance with Equity Percentages after satisfaction of outstanding encumbrances and costs.

7.7 Mortgage/Charge. No Co‑owner shall create any encumbrance over the Property or his/her interest without prior written consent of all other Co‑owners. If a loan is taken in the name of one Co‑owner that affects title, that Co‑owner shall indemnify others and ensure their interests are protected.
8. REPRESENTATIONS, WARRANTIES AND COVENANTS

8.1 General Representations. Each Co‑owner represents and warrants that he/she has full capacity and authority to enter into this Agreement; funds contributed are genuine and lawfully obtained; and there are no outstanding claims or encumbrances that would materially affect performance.

8.2 Covenants. Each Co‑owner covenants to comply with Applicable Law, not act in a manner prejudicing rights of other Co‑owners, and provide information reasonably requested by the Managing Owner for proper management.
9. INDEMNITY AND LIABILITY
9.1 Indemnity. Each Co‑owner shall indemnify and hold harmless the other Co‑owners from losses arising from his/her breach, fraud, wilful default, or misrepresentation.

9.2 Liability Cap. Except for liability arising from fraud, wilful misconduct or gross negligence, or breach of title representations, no Co‑owner shall be liable for indirect, consequential or punitive damages. Aggregate liability shall be limited to direct damages proven and, where relevant, the value of the relevant Co‑owner’s interest.
10. DEFAULT; REMEDIES

10.1 Default. If a Co‑owner fails to make a required contribution, repeatedly fails to perform obligations, or materially breaches this Agreement, other Co‑owners may give written notice specifying the breach and provide thirty (30) days to cure.

10.2 Remedies. If not cured, non‑defaulting Co‑owners may procure performance and recover costs, compel sale of the defaulting Co‑owner’s interest by judicial or agreed means, or buy out the defaulting Co‑owner at a valuation reduced by an agreed percentage (subject to Applicable Law and fairness principles).
11. CONFIDENTIALITY

11.1 Confidentiality. Each Co‑owner shall keep confidential all non‑public information concerning the Property and other Co‑owners’ affairs, except where disclosure is required by law or to professional advisors under duty of confidentiality.
11.2 Survival. Confidentiality obligations survive for three (3) years after termination.
12. TAXES AND STATUTORY COMPLIANCE

12.1 Taxes. Each Co‑owner shall individually bear taxes payable in respect of his/her share of income and proceeds. Any property taxes or statutory dues assessed on the Property shall be borne as per Clause 4.
12.2 Compliance. Co‑owners shall comply with all regulations, including tenancy laws and municipal regulations. Costs of compliance shall be paid from Property funds or by Co‑owners pro rata.
13. DISPUTE RESOLUTION
13.1 Amicable Resolution. Parties shall attempt in good faith to resolve disputes by negotiation within thirty (30) days.

13.2 Arbitration. If unresolved within thirty (30) days, disputes shall be referred to arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996. The seat of arbitration shall be {arbitrationCity}, India, and the language shall be English. The arbitral tribunal shall consist of a sole arbitrator mutually appointed; failing agreement within fourteen (14) days, each party shall appoint one arbitrator and the two appointed arbitrators shall appoint the presiding arbitrator.
13.3 Interim Relief. Nothing shall prevent any Party from seeking urgent interim relief from courts of competent jurisdiction.
14. TERM, TERMINATION AND EFFECT OF TERMINATION
14.1 Term. This Agreement shall continue until terminated by mutual agreement, by full Buy‑Out and transfer per Clause 7, sale of the Property or by operation of law.

14.2 Effect of Termination. Termination shall be without prejudice to (a) any rights or liabilities accrued prior to termination; (b) the obligations which by their nature survive termination (including indemnities, confidentiality, tax liabilities and dispute resolution); and (c) any distribution of funds or assets required by this Agreement.
15. NOTICES

Any notice, demand, consent or other communication required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered by hand, registered post, courier or email (with delivery/read receipt) to the address of the relevant Co‑owner set out in the Schedule or such other address as notified. Deemed receipt: on delivery if by hand; fifth business day after posting if registered post; second business day after courier; or on transmission date for emails (with electronic confirmation).
16. AMENDMENT; 17. SEVERABILITY; 18. WAIVER

This Agreement may be amended only by a written instrument signed by all Co‑owners. If any provision is held invalid, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force. No failure or delay by a Party in exercising any right shall operate as a waiver; a waiver must be in writing and signed.
19. GOVERNING LAW; 20. COSTS AND STAMP DUTY

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. The courts at {arbitrationCity} shall have jurisdiction to entertain actions for interim relief and enforcement of arbitral awards, subject to the arbitration clause. Each Party shall bear its own costs unless otherwise agreed. Stamp duty, registration charges and levies shall be borne and paid as set out in Clause 3.2 and Applicable Law.
21. COUNTERPARTS AND ELECTRONIC EXECUTION; 22. ENTIRE AGREEMENT

This Agreement may be executed in counterparts; electronic execution (scanned signatures, PDF copies, or other electronic methods) shall be valid and binding, and each Party agrees to deliver an original signed copy if reasonably required. This Agreement (including Schedules and Annexures) constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the Property and supersedes prior understandings.
SCHEDULE — CONTACT DETAILS AND CONTRIBUTIONS
The following details shall be maintained for each Co‑owner: Name; Address for notices; PAN/Aadhaar (optional); Contribution (INR); Equity Percentage; and Bank Details for distributions.
Co‑owners (for completion): {investorsBlock}
ANNEXURE A — PROPERTY DESCRIPTION AND TITLE DETAILS
Full legal description, survey/flat no., area, boundaries, current titleholder, encumbrances, mortgage details.
ANNEXURE B — TRANSFER PROCEDURE & BUY‑OUT NOTICE TEMPLATE
Template Transfer Notice, timeline for ROFR, valuation process, documentation checklist, sample transfer/quitclaim deed format.
ANNEXURE C — MANAGING OWNER POWERS & BANK MANDATE
Detail powers, limitations, signing rules for Property Account, reserve thresholds, emergency spending limits.
EXECUTION
IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties hereto have executed this Co‑Ownership Agreement on the date first written above.
Homebuyer/Managing Owner (if applicable): Signature __________________________  Name: {managingOwnerName}  Date: ________.
Investors: {numberOfInvestors} in number (signature pages to be appended, or sign below as applicable).
Witness 1: Name {witness1Name}  Signature __________  Address {witness1Address}.
Witness 2: Name {witness2Name}  Signature __________  Address {witness2Address}.
Note: This template is a legal-style draft for demonstration purposes. For enforceability please complete Aadhaar eSign/e-stamping and obtain legal review before relying on this Agreement.
